---
title: "Foundation Models for Multi-Study Conjoint Learning"
author: "strategize package authors"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Foundation Models for Multi-Study Conjoint Learning}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

# Overview

Foundation-model ownership is split across two packages:

- `preference.fm` trains pooled preference foundation models, writes checkpoint directories, and adapts current semantic zero-overlap models to target studies.
- `strategize` loads trained artifacts, extracts embeddings, and runs saved adapted predictors.

This keeps `strategize` focused on runtime use of trained representations. The
old `strategize::fit_conjoint_foundation_model()` and
`strategize::save_conjoint_foundation_bundle()` names are migration stubs.

# Backend Setup

Both packages use the same JAX backend. Run this once:

```{r backend, eval=FALSE}
strategize::build_backend(conda_env = "strategize_env")
```

The backend includes `orbax-checkpoint` for the Python module
`orbax.checkpoint`, which is used for JAX-native PyTree checkpoints.

# Train With preference.fm

```{r train, eval=FALSE}
library(preference.fm)

foundation_fit <- preference.fm::fit_conjoint_foundation_model(
  experiments = list(study_a, study_b),
  foundation_control = list(
    neural_mcmc_control = list(
      ModelDims = 32L,
      ModelDepth = 1L,
      subsample_method = "batch_vi",
      uncertainty_scope = "output",
      svi_steps = 100L
    )
  ),
  conda_env = "strategize_env"
)

preference.fm::save_conjoint_foundation_bundle(
  file = "foundation_bundle",
  foundation_model = foundation_fit,
  overwrite = TRUE
)
```

New artifacts are directories with `manifest.json` and an Orbax checkpoint under
`arrays/`. Legacy `.rds` foundation bundles remain loadable by `strategize`, but
new large pooled FM bundles should be written by `preference.fm`.

# Load With strategize, Adapt With preference.fm

```{r load-adapt, eval=FALSE}
library(strategize)
library(preference.fm)

foundation_fit <- strategize::load_conjoint_foundation_bundle(
  file = "foundation_bundle",
  preload_params = FALSE
)

adapted_fit <- preference.fm::adapt_conjoint_foundation_model(
  foundation_model = foundation_fit,
  Y = target_y,
  W = target_w,
  X = target_x,
  mode = "pairwise",
  pair_id = target_pair_id,
  profile_order = target_profile_order,
  experiment_id = "target_study",
  foundation_adaptation_control = list(text_embedding_fn = text_embedding_fn),
  conda_env = "strategize_env"
)

predict(
  adapted_fit,
  newdata = list(
    W = target_w,
    X = target_x,
    pair_id = target_pair_id,
    profile_order = target_profile_order
  )
)
```

Adapted semantic foundation predictors record the target-study prediction
schema, so prediction-time `X` can use the raw target covariate names.

# Embeddings

Raw loaded foundation objects can be used for embedding extraction:

```{r embeddings, eval=FALSE}
emb <- strategize::extract_embeddings(
  foundation_fit,
  newdata = list(
    W = target_w,
    pair_id = target_pair_id,
    profile_order = target_profile_order
  ),
  p_list = strategize::create_p_list(target_w, uniform = TRUE)
)
```

# Common Failure Modes

- Calling `strategize::fit_conjoint_foundation_model()` for new pooled training.
- Calling `strategize::save_conjoint_foundation_bundle()` for new artifacts.
- Loading a checkpoint directory before installing `orbax-checkpoint`.
- Calling `strategize::adapt_conjoint_foundation_model()` on current semantic zero-overlap foundation models instead of `preference.fm::adapt_conjoint_foundation_model()`.
- Attempting adaptation when the trained foundation object has no compatible group for the target study.
